threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 67 bus will get you anywhere on time. Welcome to Dalston, mate. Better get used to delays and diversions.
Caught a ride on the 196, and a guy in a chicken costume was humming 'Wonderwall.' Only in Brixton at 2 AM, I swear.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy busking with a cat on his shoulder while the ÖPNV was on strike—only in Neukölln do you find such perfect chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, you can find a bar where the drinks are only served in vintage teacups. Cheers to quirky aesthetics!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they react to BART delays. A nod of acceptance, not outrage—it's just another Friday in the Bay.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 14 Mission calmly eating a burrito while chatting with a performer playing a ukulele. Only in SF does that vibe feel perfectly normal.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think 'Gràcia' is just a one-time visit for Gaudí. They miss the tiny plaça with local artists and the bakery that’s been there since the '60s.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by their puzzled looks at the metro signs – the ones who still haven’t figured out that it’s all in Catalan.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of 'the overground' when you’re on the East London line. You'll learn soon enough.
Last night on the 133, a guy serenaded the entire bus with a ukulele. Oddly calming, but Brixton needs no more late-night talent shows!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I stumbled upon an impromptu market in the park, where everyone shared homemade goodies and swapped stories. That's when it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Friedrichshain, you'll see people doing yoga on the banks of the Spree at sunrise, surrounded by graffiti and the sounds of the city waking up.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know to avoid BART during the evening rush; the overcooked brakes are an early indicator of chaos if you're not from here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 this morning; two guys started a spontaneous rap-off while waiting. The driver just shook his head and kept it moving. Classic San Francisco.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for parties, but they miss the quiet corners where locals sip café amb llet and enjoy the morning sun.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they’re new by the way they still fumble with the t10 ticket machine—always staring confused at the screen before making a choice.
